customer_anxiety_profile: Retired, fixed income, on a landline or near-landline setup, low tech comfort. Not hostile — genuinely curious and cooperative — but passive. She will not chase the appointment if something feels off. Transfer fatigue is real ('I get transferred all day') so the closer's first 30 seconds need to feel like a continuation, not another cold call.
unresolved_concerns: Credit score unknown — Charlotte said 'I have no idea what my credit score is' and the qualifier was never resolved. Closer should be prepared to run the pull or address this before the proposal.

Bill data is incomplete — KWH readings were collected month-by-month from paper bills but November is missing and some months were ambiguous. The closer should not assume the design is fully built from this data.

Appointment format unconfirmed — Meg described an in-person visit ('come by with the design'). If the closer's format is actually a phone call, Charlotte needs to be re-briefed before the appointment or she will be expecting someone at her door.
sensitive_topics_raised: Retirement and fixed income — Charlotte volunteered this unprompted. She is cost-sensitive and the value bridge to bill certainty was never made by the setter. The closer has the opportunity to make it.
Transfer fatigue — Charlotte mentioned being transferred all day. The closer should open with continuity ('Meg told me about you') rather than re-qualifying from scratch.
exact_promises_rep_made: 'Me and my design team will get started on building your options for you right away.'
'We'll come by with the design' at 10:30 tomorrow morning (Wednesday).
Meg gave Charlotte the ION phone number (385-375-7400) and said 'ask for Meg' if she needs anything.
closer_should_know: Charlotte is cooperative but passive — she will not push back, but she also will not show up energized. The bill flip was never made: she's on a fixed income with a seasonally variable bill and no one connected those dots to solar's value for her specifically. That's the closer's opening move. Confirm the appointment format before the sit — Meg described an in-person visit, which may not match the closer's actual format.
